voice_name: George — warm, mature British narrator (closest legitimate ElevenLabs match to an Attenborough-style nature-doc read; alt: Daniel onwK4e9ZLuTAKqWW03F9)
note: Attenborough-style narration for the grayscott reveal. Sense of wonder, measured cadence, present tense. Lines align to blend_cut.py caption beats and auto-stretch to the audio length.
lines: What you are looking at appears to be a living cell, dividing.
And yet, it is not alive. No DNA. No membrane. Nothing we would call life.
It is a simulation — just two chemicals, obeying two simple rules.
And still, it grows. It divides. It behaves, for all the world, as if it were alive. Perhaps this is how life itself began.
